decision.semantic_admission_delta: positive for focused same-file direct-function Promise producer fixtures; unchanged on the local crates runtime surface because crates has no JS/TS Promise continuation rows
decision.summary: Open the proof-backed direct-function subset of the local/parameter Promise call-return queue. A direct call to a same-file function now receives PromiseLike result-domain evidence when the callee is a single-return function whose returned expression already has asserted PromiseLike domain evidence.
policy: A call result used as a Promise continuation receiver is not Promise proof by itself.
Direct-function recovery requires explicit DirectFunction call-target evidence plus returned-expression Domain(PromiseLike) evidence.
The value graph evaluates only the single returned expression for this producer path, leaving generic pure inline global-binding rules unchanged.
The recovered continuation result remains behind a Promise boundary and must not merge with synchronous payload code.

Parameter callees, member call-return receivers, imported call-return receivers, constructors, .finally, aggregate combinators, broad async/await scheduling, and unsafe thenable assimilation remain closed.

source_prevalence.scan_condition: 120-repo JS/TS lexical source scan for .then/.catch/.finally call-return receivers
source_prevalence.implemented_subset: same-file direct function callees with a single returned expression carrying PromiseLike domain proof
limitations: The source scan is a pricing signal, not proof of semantic recovery in every source occurrence.
The local/parameter source bucket mixes direct local functions and parameter/dynamic function values; this slice opens only direct local functions.
A returned Promise.resolve argument must still be non-thenable-safe before fulfillment recovery opens.
Parameter callee return-domain contracts need a separate proof path before exact admission.

producer_evidence: normalize.call_target.direct_function_return_domain emits Domain(PromiseLike) on the call result only when the target is a non-async single-return function and the returned expression has asserted PromiseLike domain evidence
the PromiseLike call-result domain evidence depends on both the DirectFunction target evidence and the returned expression's domain evidence
value_graph: Promise receiver recovery first consumes direct-function single-return producers when the receiver call has PromiseLike domain evidence
the specialized evaluator evaluates only the returned expression with arity-safe argument binding and recursion-depth guards
generic pure inlining's global-binding checks remain unchanged; this avoids broad helper inlining admission just to recover Promise producers
newly_supported_equivalences: function load() { return Promise.resolve(1); } load().then(x => x + 1) converges with Promise.resolve(1).then(x => x + 1)
function load(x: number) { return Promise.resolve(x); } load(x).then(v => v + 1) converges with Promise.resolve(x).then(v => v + 1)
function load() { return Promise.reject(1); } load().catch(e => e + 1) converges with Promise.reject(1).catch(e => e + 1)
the recovered Promise continuation stays distinct from the corresponding synchronous payload
still_closed_equivalences: function load(x) { return Promise.resolve(x); } load(x).then(v => v) does not converge with Promise.resolve(x).then(v => v) because x may be thenable
parameter callees such as factory().then(...) remain closed without a return-domain contract for factory
member, imported binding/member, dynamic dispatch, constructor, .finally, aggregate, await, and throw/rejection scheduling paths remain closed unless their own obligations are proved
reporting_delta: Promise continuation recall-loss rows no longer report promise-call-return-direct-function-return-domain-proof when a same-file DirectFunction receiver returns a proven PromiseLike expression
remaining direct-function continuation misses are attributed to continuation/channel proof, such as promise-then-fulfillment-continuation-contract-missing

covered: DirectFunction calls to single-return functions returning a proven PromiseLike expression emit PromiseLike domain evidence
the direct-function result domain does not appear without returned-expression domain proof
same-file direct function Promise.resolve return recovery converges with direct Promise.resolve.then
typed non-thenable parameters recover while untyped possible thenables stay closed
Promise.reject direct-function returns recover the catch channel
recovery does not erase the Promise boundary into synchronous payload equivalence
same-file direct Promise-returning function proof is no longer reported as missing when PromiseLike domain evidence exists
remaining Promise.then work is attributed to fulfillment/rejection/callback obligations

next_work: Add a proof path for parameter callee return-domain contracts before admitting factory().then(...) style receivers.
Price and split member call-return receiver proof separately, because the largest remaining queue is member_call_return_receiver_candidate.
Keep constructor executor, .finally settlement, aggregate combinators, rejection scheduling, and broad async/await scheduling closed until their obligations have hard negatives.
